summary: Comprehensive frontend testing for Task A (new /beyond-disclosure whitepaper) and Task B (raw-HTML prerender of 5 essay routes). ALL required assertions PASS. Raw HTML build artifacts contain the required H1s, keywords, and in-body internal links; React runtime renders correctly on the preview URL with exactly one visible H1 per route and zero console errors. Sitemap and /writing index card both present.

issues: React runtime does not set page-specific og:type/og:image via Helmet: on the live preview og:type='website' and og:image='https://thirdrailsystems.ee/og.png' (site defaults). The RAW build HTML at /app/frontend/build/beyond-disclosure.html DOES set og:type=article and og:image=/og/beyond-disclosure.png correctly, so SEO crawlers reading raw HTML are unaffected. Low priority: only impacts scrapers that execute JS.

action_items: OPTIONAL/LOW: Consider adding a React Helmet block on BeyondDisclosure.jsx to set og:type='article' and og:image='https://thirdrailsystems.ee/og/beyond-disclosure.png' at runtime, so JS-executing scrapers see the same meta as the prerender.
critical_code_review_comments: Postbuild prerender injection is well-scoped (sr-only absolute-positioned) so it does not disrupt visual layout for JS users — good pattern.

H1 uniqueness maintained across all 6 tested routes (Beyond Disclosure, catch-22, memo, the-switch, nothing-happened, exposure-is-not-democratic) — exactly 1 visible h1 per page as required by SEO spec 2026-02-12.
Runtime Helmet coverage for og:type/og:image missing on /beyond-disclosure — minor gap between raw HTML meta and runtime meta.

success_rate.frontend: 100% required assertions passing; 1 informational runtime meta gap

context_for_next_testing_agent: Task A + Task B verified. Raw HTML assertions were run directly against /app/frontend/build/*.html files (5 essays + beyond-disclosure). All required grep checks pass: (a) single sr-only <h1> with exact required text; (b) required in-body keywords present (enshittification, Y2K, diagnostic, ISO 31030, Thesis); (c) internal <a href> links: the-switch→exposure-is-not-democratic, nothing-happened→catch-22, exposure-is-not-democratic→nothing-happened+diagnostic. Raw beyond-disclosure.html meta: title, canonical=thirdrailsystems.ee/beyond-disclosure, og:type=article, og:image=/og/beyond-disclosure.png, og:description present. Preview URL React runtime: whitepaper visible with H1 'Beyond Disclosure', dek, 'Levi Hankins · Founder & CEO', 7 headings (Abstract, 5 numbered, Appendix), zero console errors. /writing index card data-testid='writing-index-card-beyond-disclosure' points to /beyond-disclosure. Sitemap.xml (served by preview) contains https://thirdrailsystems.ee/beyond-disclosure. Only informational finding: React runtime does not override og:type/og:image for beyond-disclosure — cosmetic since crawlers use raw HTML.

writing/nothing-happened.html.h1_text: Nothing Happened, and That Was the Point

catch-22.html.h1_text: The ISO 31030 and GDPR Article 9 Catch-22

rca of the issue: N/A — no failing requirements. Informational only: React runtime for /beyond-disclosure doesn't override site-level og:type/og:image meta tags in Helmet; the raw-HTML prerender (crawler-facing) has correct meta. Fix would be to add page-specific <Helmet> in /app/frontend/src/pages/BeyondDisclosure.jsx setting og:type='article' and og:image='https://thirdrailsystems.ee/og/beyond-disclosure.png'.
